faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Is it safe to convert private images to PDF here?
faq.a1: Absolutely. Your AVIF to PDF conversion happens entirely in your browser. Images are never uploaded to any external server — your files stay on your device at all times.
faq.q2: Will the PDF preserve original image quality?
faq.a2: Yes. The AVIF image is embedded in the PDF at full resolution. The image quality in the PDF matches the original AVIF source with no additional compression.
faq.q3: How do I open AVIF files on Windows?
faq.a3: Windows 11 supports AVIF natively. On Windows 10, install the free 'AV1 Video Extension' from the Microsoft Store. Or simply use Avifkit to convert your AVIF files to PDF for easy viewing and printing.
faq.q4: Can I open AVIF on iPhone or Mac?
faq.a4: macOS Ventura and iOS 16+ support AVIF natively. For older Apple devices, converting AVIF to PDF with Avifkit is the easiest way to view and share your images.
faq.q5: Can I print the PDF directly?
faq.a5: Yes. The generated PDF is print-ready. Open it in any PDF reader — Adobe Acrobat, Preview on Mac, Chrome, or Edge — and print directly at full resolution.
faq.q6: What page size will the PDF use?
faq.a6: The PDF page size automatically matches the dimensions of your AVIF image, ensuring no cropping or stretching occurs.
faq.q7: What is the maximum file size I can convert?
faq.a7: Each file can be up to 10MB. Since processing happens entirely in your browser, larger files may take slightly longer depending on your device's performance.
faq.q8: Can I batch convert AVIF files to PDF?
faq.a8: Yes. Upload up to 30 AVIF files at once. Each will be converted to a separate PDF document. Download them individually or as a ZIP archive.
faq.a9: AVIF (AV1 Image File Format) is a modern image format that offers superior compression compared to JPG and WebP. It is supported by Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari 16.4+, but not all applications can open it — converting to PDF ensures universal compatibility.
faq.q10: Do I need to create an account?
faq.a10: No. Avifkit is completely free with no registration, no watermarks, and no usage limits. Just open the page and start converting.
faq.q11: Can I combine multiple AVIF images into one PDF?
faq.a11: Currently, each AVIF image is converted to a separate PDF document. For combining multiple images into a single multi-page PDF, convert them individually and use a PDF merge tool.
